meaning: These letters begin this chapter and are pronounced as their full names: Alif, Lam, Meem
and he will definitely know (Allah will know those who are truthful)
they do/work (referring to those who do evil deeds in this verse)
they judge (referring to those who do evil deeds in this ayah)
and We will surely reward them
they both strove intensely against you
then I will inform you all
we will surely cause them to enter
with more knowledge (of what's in the hearts of all beings)
and surely he will definitely know
they (the disbelievers who are not carrying sins)
capable of carrying/bearing (in context: they are not capable of bearing)
and surely he will carry/bear (their burdens)
and he will most certainly be asked
and all of you fear/be mindful of Him
so you all strive to seek
and thank! (command to all of you)
the clear message (that must be conveyed)
that (in this ayah, refers to the resurrection/bringing back to life mentioned before)
you all are being turned over
by/with those who make others powerless (describing how they are not able to escape Allah's plan)
any strong protector (in context: besides Allah)
and those (referring to those who disbelieved)
they said (the response of his people)
Allah (as the doer who saved him)
and he said (introducing his statement about idols)
the worldly life (in contrast to the afterlife)
he (referring to Allah in this ayah)
The Mighty One (a name/attribute of Allah)
the righteous ones (among whom he will be)
to his people (who Lut spoke to)
indeed you all (speaking to Lot's people)
surely you all come (addressing multiple males)
he went ahead of you all
is it true that you all
the village (that is being discussed in this story)
in it/her (referring to the city where Lut was)
Lot (as the object being talked about)
they said (referring to the angels speaking to Ibrahim about Lut)
in it (referring to the city of Lut's people)
the ones who stay behind (those who remain after others leave)
they use reason (referring to people who should understand the clear signs)
Shuaib (as the object being sent to Madyan)
you all act wickedly (in a corrupting way)
people who damage and make things worse
so they denied him (referring to the messenger mentioned earlier)
so they became/entered into a state (after the earthquake struck them)
ones who are lying flat/crouching motionless
and among them (referring to the previous groups mentioned)
a thing (that they call upon besides Allah)
and that (feminine, pointing to those examples)
except (in the ayah: and none comprehends them except the learned)
with the truth (describing how Allah created the heavens and earth)
that (referring to Allah's creation of the heavens and earth)
you all make/create (in the ayah: what you all do/make)
those who (referring to some of the People of the Book)
from them (referring to those who wronged/did injustice)
to you (receiving what We sent down)
the disbelievers (those who reject Our signs)
Rather (introduces what follows as contrasting with what was mentioned before)
the wrongdoers (those who reject Allah's signs)
upon them (referring to those who doubt in the previous ayah)
they believe (referring to those who receive the message)
and they seek to rush you
and surely he will definitely come to them
Hell (as the object being surrounded in this verse)
with/by the ones who reject/cover up truth
so (it is) specifically me (that you should worship)
so worship Me! (command to many people)
surely we will cause them to settle
the garden/paradise (referring to the eternal garden in heaven)
upper rooms/chambers (as the object being given to believers)
the rivers (that flow under the gardens)
The All-Hearing (one of Allah's names, subject of the sentence)
you asked them (referring to a group being asked)
then how (are they deluded away from this truth)
they are turned away (from the truth despite knowing Allah created everything)
who (with emphasis, asking about the one who sent down water from the sky)
water (as the object being sent down)
and then he gave life to
they will surely say (in response to being asked who sends down rain)
and what/not (connects this question/negation to previous statement)
Allah (as the object being called upon)
when (in the ayah: when they ride/embark)
and so that they may enjoy themselves
and have they not...? (questioning the past)
and he is being snatched away repeatedly
the people (as the doers in this ayah)
Is it then with falsehood (that they believe)?
or (connecting two parts: 'invents a lie' or 'denies the truth')
